context: Tests whether the agent implements category move operations with transactional subtree path updates, and breadcrumb retrieval using materialized path_ids rather than recursive queries. The agent is given an existing category structure and asked to implement reorganization and breadcrumb features.

checklist.name: Transaction wraps all updates
checklist.description: The moveCategory implementation wraps all database writes (the moved category AND its descendants) inside a single database transaction

checklist.name: Descendants found via path prefix
checklist.description: Finding descendants uses a 'path starts with' / prefix query (e.g. WHERE path LIKE 'old/path/%' or { path: { startsWith: category.path + '/' } }) rather than a recursive CTE or multiple round-trips

checklist.name: Descendant paths rewritten
checklist.description: After a category move, descendant rows have their 'path' field updated (via string replacement of old prefix with new prefix)

checklist.name: Descendant pathIds rewritten
checklist.description: After a category move, descendant rows have their 'path_ids' (pathIds) array updated to reflect the new ancestry

checklist.name: depth recalculated on move
checklist.description: The 'depth' field is recalculated for the moved category and its descendants after a move

checklist.name: Breadcrumb single query
checklist.description: The breadcrumb retrieval function fetches all ancestors in a single DB query using the path_ids array (e.g. WHERE id IN (path_ids)), NOT a recursive query or one-by-one lookups

checklist.name: Self excluded from ancestors
checklist.description: The ancestor query excludes the current category itself (e.g. using .slice(0, -1) or excluding own ID)

checklist.name: Ancestors ordered by depth
checklist.description: Ancestors are ordered by 'depth' ascending so breadcrumbs are returned root-first

checklist.name: URL uses /c/ prefix
checklist.description: Generated breadcrumb URLs use the '/c/' prefix followed by the category path (e.g. '/c/clothing/women/dresses')

checklist.name: No recursive CTE
checklist.description: Neither the move nor the breadcrumb logic uses a recursive SQL CTE or WITH RECURSIVE query

checklist.name: Tree ordered by depth then position
checklist.description: The full category tree query orders results by depth ascending first, then by position ascending
